# @zakkster/lite-headless / affix

Pin an element to the viewport (or a scroll container) when its
natural top reaches an offset. The "sticky header that latches in
place once you scroll past it" pattern, done without `position:
sticky`'s containment quirks.

## How it works

Inserts a 1px-tall sentinel div right before the target. An
`IntersectionObserver` watches the sentinel with a negative top-
margin equal to `offsetTop`. When the sentinel "leaves" that margin-
shrunk viewport, the target is pinned.

This avoids:
- `position: sticky` containment issues with `overflow` ancestors
- Scroll-listener layout reads on the main thread
- Re-running on every scroll tick (IO is browser-native throttled)

## Quick example

```js
import { createAffix } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/affix";

const aff = createAffix({
    offsetTop: 64,      // pin once 64px from viewport top
    root: null,         // null = viewport; pass an Element for a scroll container
    onChange: (pinned) => {},
});

aff.attachRoot(navEl);  // the element that gets pinned
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `offsetTop` | `0` | px from viewport (or root) top at which to pin |
| `root` | `null` (viewport) | Scroll container element |
| `onChange` | - | `(pinned: boolean) => void` |

## API

```ts
{
    isPinned(): boolean
    offsetTop(): number

    attachRoot(targetEl): off
    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

### Target (`[data-affix-root]`)

- `data-pinned` — boolean, true while pinned

### Sentinel (auto-injected `[data-affix-sentinel]`)

- `aria-hidden="true"` — decoration only
- Inline styles: `position:relative; height:0; margin:0; padding:0; border:0;`
  (1px in concept, 0px in layout so it doesn't push siblings)

## Wrapper (`<lite-affix>`)

```html
<lite-affix offset-top="64">
    <nav class="page-nav">...</nav>
</lite-affix>

<!-- Inside a scroll container -->
<div id="scroll-area" style="overflow:auto; height: 500px;">
    <lite-affix offset-top="0" root="#scroll-area">
        <h2>Section header</h2>
    </lite-affix>
    ...long content...
</div>
```

Attributes:
- `offset-top` — px (default 0)
- `root` — CSS selector for scroll container; defaults to viewport

Event: `affixchange` — `{ detail: { pinned } }`

## CSS contract

```css
[data-affix-root] {
    transition: box-shadow 180ms;
}
[data-affix-root][data-pinned] {
    position: sticky;
    top: 0;
    z-index: 10;
    background: var(--surface);
    box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
}
```

Note: the primitive doesn't itself apply `position: sticky`. It only
paints the state attribute. Consumer chooses sticky / fixed / class
swap based on layout needs.
